reasoning: Phase 4 Feature 2 API Q&A tools is correctly implemented. Invariant 1 (R1.1 EXTENDED): OhanaClient.call() writes verified user_id AND shop_id into the body LAST, guaranteeing override of any smuggled params — test_call_verified_ids_override_smuggled_params confirms adversarial injection fails. Invariant 2 (verify=True hardcoded): TLS verification is hardcoded on the lazy httpx.AsyncClient path; no code toggles it. Invariant 3 (method-name regex): _METHOD_RE [a-z0-9_]+ enforced before any HTTP call; test_call_rejects_bad_method_name confirms /, ., uppercase all rejected. Invariant 4 (bridge exceptions → failure envelope): all OhanaError subclasses caught in tools/ohana_read.py and translated to {success: False, error: <code>} via _classify_error; orchestrator never sees raw exceptions. Invariant 5 (no secret logging): service_key and params never logged; only method, shop_id, user_id, latency_ms, status appear. Invariant 6 (handler signature): build_order_status_tool returns Tool with handler matching ToolHandler contract (user_id, shop_id, args → dict), kind='read' correct. All 10 tests pass: happy path, smuggling override, status=false, 401/429, malformed JSON, path injection, tool success, error envelope, input validation. Handler input validation (empty order_id → {success: False}) runs before bridge call. PRE-002 (real platform API spec) is unresolved but acknowledged in ALLOWED_FILES/spec; gate is contract-shape only via MockTransport. No financial integrity risks; no wallet/transaction/balance/commission logic touched. Tenant boundary protection works via (user_id, shop_id) identity pair forwarding.
